title: ceraia papilionifera
intro: Ceraia papilionifera, also known as the butterfly tree, is a plant species native to tropical forests in Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its active compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ceraia papilionifera include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat wounds, as well as its ability to support immune function and protect against oxidative stress. Ceraia papilionifera is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
